title: I let this thought through without grabbing it
subtitle: A short affirmation for practising non-attachment to recurring thoughts.
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 14 of the SLEEP program. One sentence for the moment a familiar thought arrives — used to practise letting it pass rather than engaging, without suppressing or fighting it.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 14 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798269649795).

practice.whenToUse: Use any time a thought loop starts — day or night. Say it once, at the moment of noticing, as a conscious alternative to following the thought.

content.text: A short affirmation from Day 14 of the SLEEP program. The sentence does something specific: it acknowledges the thought's presence while declining to follow it. Not suppression — passage. The thought comes through and keeps moving.
I let this thought through without grabbing it.

Say it three times in the morning as a rehearsal. Then use it in real time: the thought appears (the 3am loop, the tomorrow-worry, the unresolved conversation) and you say the sentence once. You are not arguing with the thought or blocking it. You are simply choosing not to pick it up.
Adapted from Day 14 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
